note_en: Arena Autopilot — two simulated $10,000 ledgers (Model A: intraday double-window swing; Model B: end-of-day deep review) trading a fixed universe (public/arena-universe.json) under code-enforced risk rules (public/../src/lib/arenaRules.js — the model proposes, the rules engine settles, never the other way around). Not investment advice.
note_zh: Arena Autopilot——两本各 $10,000 的模拟账本（Model A：日内双窗口 swing；Model B：盘后深度复盘），在固定交易域内（public/arena-universe.json）按代码强制的风控规则运作（src/lib/arenaRules.js——模型只提案，规则引擎收单，永远不是反过来）。非投资建议。

review.zh: 半导体/存储今日普涨反弹（NVDA+4%、MU+4.9%、SNDK+5%），KeyBanc上调美光目标价至1750美元强化HBM/DRAM供给紧张逻辑；但企业软件因IBM二季度预警重挫（ADBE-4.3%、CRM-2.1%），预警指向企业资本开支从软件转向硬件。践行上次反思：不因单日利空追加卖出CRM（未触及止损线），也不追高昨日刚止损离场的MU，避免来回打脸交易。AMD、NVDA维持不动，跟随板块动能。
review.en: Chips/memory rebounded broadly today (NVDA +4%, MU +4.9%, SNDK +5%) after KeyBanc raised its Micron target to $1750 on tighter DRAM/HBM supply, while enterprise software sold off on IBM's Q2 warning that budgets are shifting from software to hardware (ADBE -4.3%, CRM -2.1%). Discipline check: not selling CRM on one bad headline day (nowhere near stop), and not chasing back into MU one day after exiting it at a loss. Holding AMD/NVDA/CRM unchanged.

review.zh: 本次运行遇到报价接口配置问题：仅10/30标的（核心AI硬件+SMH）成功取得报价，持仓中的MSFT、GOOGL及其余大盘科技/基准标的均无法取价，需人工核查报价工具配置。缺乏持仓现价，无法做出可靠的减仓判断；即便今日是可开仓窗口（周二），also缺乏对大盘科技板块的有效报价支持新建仓提案。新闻面：AI硬件板块本周大幅下挫（半导体指数年内仍+47%但短期修正逾万亿美元市值），微软下财年资本开支上调至1900亿美元引发自由现金流担忧，Alphabet同步上调指引至1800-1900亿美元且获市场正面反馈。数据缺口下选择空仓不动，维持低换手率纪律，等待下次报价恢复正常后再评估。
review.en: This run hit a quote-fetch config issue: only 10/30 universe symbols (core AI-hardware + SMH) returned prices; MSFT and GOOGL, both current holdings, plus the rest of megacap-tech/benchmarks, could not be priced — needs a manual check on the quote tool config. Without current position prices I can't make a reliable sell call, and even though today is a valid Tue open-window, I lack price coverage to justify a new buy. Context: AI-hardware sold off sharply this week despite +47% YTD; Microsoft raised FY26 capex guidance to $190B (raising FCF concerns) while Alphabet raised its own capex guide to $180-190B and was rewarded by the market. Given the data gap, doing nothing and preserving low-turnover discipline until pricing is restored.
